title: Federal Reserve signals policy uncertainty remains elevated
summary: Federal Reserve communications suggest policymakers still see inflation progress as uneven. Markets may continue repricing rate expectations as new macro data arrives.
key_points: Inflation progress was described as uneven
Rate expectations may stay sensitive to incoming data
why_it_matters: This matters because Fed tone can affect yields, risk appetite, and positioning across equities, bonds, and other macro-sensitive assets.
macro_context: The market is still highly sensitive to inflation and labor data because both affect expected policy paths.
market_structure_context: Treasury yields and rate-sensitive positioning can move quickly when Fed communication changes expected policy timing.

issues_found: Market impact language may be slightly too broad
Source may need human confirmation for interpretation strength
